name: Fig Bars
recipeInstructions:
1. For the filling: finely chop figs and mix in a saucepan with sugar, water, and lemon juice.
2. Place over medium heat; cook, stirring for 10 minutes or until thick and jam-like. Remove from heat and let cool. Cover and chill.
3. With a mixer, beat together butter, brown sugar, eggs, and vanilla until well blended.
4. Mix flour, salt, and baking soda in a separate bowl.
5. Stir the dry ingredients into the cooled filling; the dough will be stiff.
6. Wrap in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 3-4 hours or overnight.
7. When ready to bake, turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface.
8. Roll into a 14x12 rectangle.
9. Cut into 4 strips that are 3-1/2 x 12 inches long.
10. Spoon the filling evenly down the center of each strip.
11. With a spatula, fold in the sides of the strips.
12. Press edges together to seal.
13. Cut each strip into 10 pieces.
14. Arrange seam-side down on baking sheets.
15. Bake at 375°F for 10-12 minutes or until firm and lightly browned.
16. Cool on racks. Store airtight.
